assumptions: The developer has a supported browser, email address, and a local application workspace.
No Stytch account, workspace, project, or SDK integration exists yet.

developer_goal: Create a Stytch account and project, implement an official authentication path, and complete a login or signup that produces an authenticated session.

first_success.outcome: The application completes a Stytch login or signup and receives session and user data.
first_success.observable_signal: After the user completes the selected Stytch UI or API flow, the application can read an authenticated session and user object.

route_selection.surface: Stytch Authentication Quickstart and Consumer login-or-signup guides
route_selection.rule: Select one implementation type, framework, and Consumer versus Multi-tenant project type only after those peer choices are fixed; do not invent a universal Stytch path.

candidates.name: Stytch UI with React Consumer Authentication
candidates.reason: Prebuilt UI minimizes custom auth UI code, but the Quickstart still presents Stytch UI, Headless, and Backend-only as peer implementation types and Consumer versus Multi-tenant as peer project types.

candidates.name: Headless frontend SDK
candidates.reason: Peer route for developers who own their UI; not ranked below Stytch UI by the general Quickstart.

candidates.name: Backend-only Consumer Authentication
candidates.reason: Peer API/SDK route with different credentials and no frontend UI component path.

candidates.name: Multi-tenant Authentication
candidates.reason: Peer project type for organization/member apps; changes dashboard configuration and SDK surfaces.

required_path.action: Sign up for a Stytch account and create a project through the Stytch Dashboard.

required_fields.label: Dashboard signup and initial project fields
required_fields.notes: API Keys docs require an account to obtain keys, but the public dashboard does not expose the signup field set in documentation.

required_path.observable_result: The developer has a Stytch project with environment credentials.

required_path.action: Enable Frontend SDKs, authorize the local development domain, and copy the Test public token.
required_path.observable_result: Frontend SDK calls from the local app domain are authorized.

required_path.action: Install the selected framework SDK, initialize the provider with the public token, and render the selected login or signup surface.

required_fields.label: Framework and implementation-specific config
required_fields.notes: React, Next.js, and other framework guides diverge after the shared public_token prerequisite.

required_path.observable_result: The local app exposes a Stytch login or signup experience.

required_path.action: Complete a login or signup through the integrated Stytch experience and confirm session and user data.

required_fields.label: Auth-method-specific user inputs
required_fields.notes: Example configs include email magic links and OAuth providers; required inputs depend on enabled products.

required_path.observable_result: The application receives authenticated session and user data.

prerequisites.description: A Stytch account is required before project credentials are available.
A local application domain must be added to Authorized Domains for Frontend SDK flows.

external_gates.description: Email magic link or OAuth provider flows introduce external mailbox or identity-provider authorization when those products are enabled.

unavoidable_waits.description: Email magic link authentication waits for message delivery when that product is selected.

platform_outcomes.description: Stytch issues project-scoped Test and Live credentials after project creation.
Successful login or signup yields session and user objects to the SDK.

excluded.item: Reading Quickstart documentation as a measured action
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is evidence, not a product action.
Migrations assume an existing identity system and are outside first-account success.

sources.title: Quickstart
evidence_supported: Entry to authentication quickstart and implementation selection

sources.title: Add login or signup
evidence_supported: Session and user data handling terminal

sources.title: Stytch Dashboard
evidence_supported: Authenticated dashboard entry; public signup field set not fully enumerable from docs

uncertainties.question: Which implementation type, framework, Consumer versus Multi-tenant project type, and auth products define the selected Stytch first-success route?
uncertainties.impact: These peer choices change dashboard configuration, SDK packages, required fields, external gates, and whether the terminal is Stytch UI session data or a backend API response.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Fix one bounded developer goal, then verify its complete current Quickstart against a fresh Dashboard project walkthrough.

uncertainties.question: What exact signup, verification, workspace, and initial-project fields does a fresh Stytch Dashboard account require?
uncertainties.impact: Account-creation field counts and any email or plan gates remain unknown, so the path cannot be verified from account creation.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Dated screenshots or a read-only walkthrough of public signup through first project credentials.
